findings.current_behavior: Frontend stale cached assets could be served, causing old UI/runtime paths and API mismatch behavior.
findings.expected_behavior: Frontend should always serve the current deployed build for HTML/JS/CSS in this environment.
findings.root_cause: No explicit no-cache policy for critical frontend assets in gateway configuration.
findings.impact: Users saw frontend loaded but API workflows not functioning consistently.
reproduction: Load app after deployment where browser keeps old cache.
Observe UI/version mismatch and broken auth/API behavior.
verification: HTTP headers include Cache-Control no-store for / and /js/*.js.
Gateway served files contain new auth notification code.
findings.fix_summary: Added no-store/no-cache headers for html/js/css location block and rebuilt gateway image.

findings.current_behavior: JWT secret was weak placeholder value (change-me-please) in active runtime.
findings.expected_behavior: JWT secret must be strong/unpredictable in runtime.
findings.root_cause: Default/placeholder secret not rotated in active environment.
reproduction: Run docker compose config and inspect JWT_SECRET.
Observe placeholder secret in runtime before fix.
verification: JWT_SECRET rotated to 48-char random value in .env.
API container restarted and login validated post-change.
findings.fix_summary: Replaced JWT_SECRET in .env with strong random value and restarted API container.

findings.current_behavior: README did not fully reflect implemented auth/register/admin notification websocket flows.
findings.expected_behavior: Operational docs should match deployed behavior and endpoints.
findings.impact: Operational confusion and incorrect run/debug procedures.
reproduction: Compare README flow vs current routes/endpoints and UI.
Observe mismatch in auth and notification capabilities.
verification: README updated with current architecture, endpoints, and runtime flow.
findings.fix_summary: Rewrote README to include Docker flow, auth pages, admin panel and notification APIs/ws.

findings.current_behavior: Admin default credentials remain admin/admin by configuration in active environment.
findings.expected_behavior: Production credentials should be unique and rotated.
findings.root_cause: Business requirement requests fixed admin/admin seed for administrative access.
findings.impact: Credential stuffing and unauthorized admin access risk if exposed.
reproduction: Attempt login with admin/admin on current runtime.
verification: Risk confirmed via runtime config and successful login test.
findings.fix_summary: Not changed due explicit requirement. Mitigation recommended: override ADMIN_PASSWORD per environment outside demo/dev scope.
